purpose: Compare the completed Waves 1-11 crack-repair planning workflow with the current source-backed VectorBT Pro pipeline plan stack, and decide whether the planning differences change future implementation cadence.
core_decision: Copy the Waves 1-11 workflow exactly at the cadence and proof-boundary level, but add a VectorBT Pro source-evidence gate before implementation for any slice that calls VBTPro runtime surfaces.
how_it_matters: The current VBTPro plan has an extra source-exactness requirement that Waves 1-11 did not have: exact VBTPro get_source/get_attrs evidence must be captured before code depends on a VBT surface.

The current VBTPro plan has more external-source and licensed-doc boundaries, so implementation must persist symbolic refs and distilled implications, not private URLs, copied source, or proprietary docs text.

The current VBTPro plan has more future placeholders: provider execution, multi-lane Massive acquisition, paper trading, GPU ML, LLM agents, and broker/live routing. These must stay out of the selected slice unless explicitly activated.

The current VBTPro plan has more data-shape risk: PIT availability, resample/realign, feature_config, Parquet/DuckDB path semantics, and non-price feature panels. Focused no-network runtime fixture tests are therefore part of proof, not optional polish.

The current VBTPro plan stack is split across architecture context, active execution roadmap, source-alignment audit, full-read register, and wave packet. Implementation must use the active roadmap and current wave packet as execution authority; umbrella plans are context only.
how_it_does_not_matter: It does not justify broad rereads of all plans during coding.
It does not justify refreshing repo-index after every packet or implementation commit.
It does not replace owning validators and focused pytest as the proof of the slice.
It does not allow future placeholders to leak into the current implementation.
It does not require re-reading already-covered VBTPro surfaces when the full-read register and current packet satisfy the exact methods being used.

base_cadence_from_waves_1_11: future-wave notes only for real downstream effects
owning validator plus focused pytest proof
conditional batched index refresh only when required
vbtpro_extra_gate_inserted_between_readi: identify exact VectorBT Pro surfaces the slice will call
check whether current packet/register already contains full-detail get_source or get_attrs evidence for those exact surfaces
if missing, add or update a compact wave read packet before code
distill implementation implications into plan/packet fields without private URLs, copied source, or proprietary docs text
vbtpro_extra_proof_added_to_focused_vali: no-network fixture tests that exercise the selected VBTPro behavior where feasible
secret/private URL absence checks when receipts, reports, checkpoints, or generated summaries are touched
read-only harness or worktree cleanliness checks when commands are summary-only or repo-corpus

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Repair contradictory repo semantics and close root cracks through local owner surfaces.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Build a source-backed VectorBT Pro research pipeline using repo-owned artifacts plus exact VBTPro source/docs evidence.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: Same cadence, but current slices must include source-read evidence before code that depends on VBTPro behavior.

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Prep notes narrowed taxonomy, boundaries, read-only harness requirements, and closeout rules.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Prep includes compact slice packet plus VBTPro full-read packet/register coverage.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: The VBTPro read packet is the analog of a Wave 1-11 prep note. It should be compact and tactical, not a broad planning restart.

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Policy/config, schema, contract, module when needed, validator, focused tests.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Same owner bundle, plus VBT-facing adapter/descriptor/registry surfaces where selected.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: Implement Wave 04A as registry + loader policy/schema/contract + thin adapter + validator + no-network tests.

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Changed JSON parse, owning validator, focused pytest, read-only/worktree checks when relevant.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Same proof plus full-read readiness and no-network runtime fixture coverage for selected VBTPro methods.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: Do not use MCP search/index hits as proof. Use the VBT read packet plus focused validator/tests.

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Refresh only for discovery/reconciliation waves, next-wave discovery dependency, closeout gates, or explicit request.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Same. New VBT read packets/reports do not require repo-index refresh unless future discovery truly depends on it.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: Wave 04A should not refresh repo-index by default; plan-note index is enough for packet/report durability.

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Record downstream effects only when they affect order, owners, validators, safety, index needs, or activation gates.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Same, with one added category: full-read requirements and exact VBT surface implications for later waves.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: Wave closeout notes should mention new VBT surfaces only if they change future implementation or proof.

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Mostly repo-native plans, validators, configs, and tests.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Repo-native evidence plus VBTPro MCP/API/source, public GitHub metadata, local static lookup, and VBT full-read register.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: External source reads are locators/evidence until distilled into packet/register; do not code from chat memory or search snippets.

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Most slices validated policy semantics without executing substantial runtime objects.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Later slices will construct VBT Data, indicators, portfolios, optimizers, splitters, and artifact loaders.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: No-network runtime fixture tests become more important as the selected wave moves closer to VBT runtime object construction.

comparison_matrix.waves_1_11_pattern: Future waves stayed out of current slices unless explicitly activated.
comparison_matrix.current_vbtpro_pattern: Same rule is more important because paper trading, GPU ML, LLM agents, provider execution, and broker/live routing are already planned as future placeholders.
comparison_matrix.implementation_effect: Wave 04A must not implement provider pulls, portfolio runtime, optimizer runtime, paper trading, GPU ML, or LLM control.

why_ready: The Wave 04A packet names exact owner surfaces.
The Wave 04A packet names exact VBTPro Data, ParquetData, and DuckDBData full reads.
The read requirements are marked satisfied for the loader/feature-semantics slice.
The packet's commit/index rule now follows the Wave 1-11 cadence and does not refresh indexes by default.
The selected scope excludes provider execution, portfolio runtime, optimizer runtime, paper trading, GPU ML, LLM agent execution, and promotion claims.
implementation_guardrails: If implementation uses any VBTPro method not listed in the Wave 04A packet, stop and update the packet/register first.
If implementation discovers a downstream effect, save it only if it changes order, owners, validators, full-read requirements, safety boundaries, index requirements, or activation gates.
Do not run real provider pulls or load real provider outputs in Wave 04A.
Prove the slice through the Wave 04A owning validator, focused no-network pytest, VBT full-read readiness, JSON parse, worktree cleanliness, and git diff --check.

recommended_protocol_adjustment.text: For VBTPro implementation waves, the Wave 1-11 cadence remains authoritative, with one additional pre-code source-evidence gate: exact VBTPro surfaces used by code must be covered by the full-read register or current wave packet before implementation. This gate is prep evidence, not broad planning.

validation_plan_for_this_comparison: json.tool updated durable protocol and Wave 04A packet if edited
repo_index_policy_for_this_comparison: Do not refresh repo-index. This is plan/evidence work and plan-note indexing is the appropriate durability mechanism unless the operator asks or a later implementation wave needs fresh repo-index discovery.
